program_en: B.Ed. in Physical Education, Exercise Science and Health
program_chi: 健康與體育運動科學教育學士
learning_outcomes:
PESHN_Learning Outcomes_ENG 1.  |  Major Programme:
Graduates of the B.Ed. Programme in Physical
Education, Exercise Science and Health (PESH) will be able to:
(1)  |  effectively perform
and advance knowledge and professional skills in physical education, exercise
science and health; (K/S)
(2)  |  master interpersonal
and organization skills in the teaching and administration of physical and
health education, as well as in sports training and coaching; (K/S)
(3)  |  apply knowledge and
skills learned from the field of physical education and exercise science
towards advancement of human health, performance and quality of life through
effective education, promotion, evaluation, research and development; (K/S)
(4)  |  demonstrate leadership
in administrative and managerial roles in the physical education, sports and
health-related sectors; (K/S/V)
(5)  |  display an informed,
critical, and innovative approach to learning; (K/S/V)
(6)  |  demonstrate an
ability and readiness to embark on creative and new ventures of social value,
in line with global and ethical values as professional teachers and leaders; (K/S/V)
and
(7)  |  continue building
upon their knowledge and skills after graduation, with an interest in
learning as a lifelong process. (K/S/V)
K = Knowledge outcomes S = Skills
outcomes V = Values and attitude outcomes
2.  |  Minor Programme:
Upon the completion of the Minor Programme,
students will have demonstrated basic understanding of fundamental issues
related to the subject, acquired knowledge of some aspects of the subject
with sufficient exposure and at a suitable level to allow further study in
this subject or related subjects or to pursue a related career.
study_scheme:
Physical Education, Exercise Science and Health Physical Education, Exercise Science and Health Applicable to students admitted in 2023-24
Major Programme Requirement
Students are required to complete a
minimum of 104 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Faculty Package:  |  9
EDUC2120, 2312, SPED2720
2.  |  Required Courses:
(a)  |  Core Courses in Physical Education, Exercise Science and
Health  |  44
PHPC1001, 2016#, SPED2520, 2540, 2600, 2610, 2710, 2810, 3410, 3430, 3440,
3450, 3820, 3920, 4570
(b)  |  Core Courses in Professional Skills  |  13
SPED2011, 2021, 2031, 2041, 2051, 2061, 2111, 2122, 2131,
2141, 2211, 3220, 3250
(c)  |  Courses in Educational Studies  |  10
EDUC2240, 3201, 3311, 4340
(d)  |  Teaching Practice  |  8
SPED3400[a], 4400[a]
(e)  |  Internship  |  2
SPED4201[a]
(f)  |  Research Experience  |  6
SPED3630[a], 4900[a]
3.  |  Elective Courses:
(a)  |  Theoretical Courses in Physical Education, Exercise Science
and Health  |  9
Three courses chosen from the following:
CHPR4012#, PHPC2007#, 2009#, SPED3550,
3731, 3910, 4560, 4640, 4830
(b)  |  Courses in Professional Skills  |  1
One course chosen from the following:
SPED2112, 2134
(c)  |  Courses in Educational Studies  |  2
One course chosen from the following:
EDUC2210, 2220, 3140, 3150, 3200, 3260, 3270, 3280, 3290,
4110, 4131, 4320, 4330
Total:  |  104
Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  SPED and EDUC courses at 2000 and
above level as included in the Major Programme Requirement as well as
those labeled as # will be included in the calculation of Major GPA for
honours classification.
2.  |  Students who have completed 14 units of Professional Skills
Courses will be eligible to apply for exemption of 2 units of University
Core Physical Education Requirement. Students are required to apply
for the exemption. When exemption from a particular course is recognized,
students can only be exempted from the course but not the units.

Minor Programme Requirement
Students are required to complete a minimum of 18 units of
courses, with at least 6 units at 3000 or above level, as follows:
Units
